methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc

data.narrative:
QuamCore is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alon Cohen that has raised $35M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 total comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion. The peer universe is narrow and heterogeneous across technology approaches and maturity stages, limiting the applicability of traditional comps-based valuation methods.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($35M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields $70M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables; the quantum computing peer set contains only one company at an adjacent stage, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum threshold required for reliable median calculation. Attempting to force an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise given the technical and commercial heterogeneity across quantum approaches.

The single applicable method supports a median valuation of $70M. Sensitivity around this point—accounting for execution risk and stage-specific uncertainty—establishes a range of $60M to $80M. Key uncertainties include technology validation timelines, competitive positioning within fragmented quantum modalities, and the path to commercialization for early-stage quantum hardware platforms. Additional financial data on burn rate and capital efficiency would refine this range meaningfully.
